item_text: this activity diagram models the login and registration process for an application.

item_text: the main flow starts with the app being launched ("avvio app").
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-login-registration-010 title: user registration and module: source pages: preconditions: test data: test steps: expected result:

item_text: it checks if the user is successfully registered ("utente registrato con successo al servizio?").
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-login-registration-010 title: user registration and module: source pages: preconditions: test data: test steps: expected result:

item_text: if the user is both registered and logged in, it leads to the homepage.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-user-002 title: user login module: user source pages: 1 preconditions: user is registered. test data: - email: user@example.com - password: password123 test steps: 1. open the application. 2. click on the login button. 3. enter the provided email and password. 4. submit the form. expected result: the user should be logged in successfully and redirected to the home page.

item_text: if the user is not registered, it proceeds to create a new user.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-user-001 title: user registration module: user source pages: 1 preconditions: test data: - email: user@example.com - password: password123 - username: testuser test steps: 1. open the application. 2. click on the registration button. 3. enter the provided email, password, and username. 4. submit the form. expected result: the user should be registered successfully and redirected to the login page.

item_text: if the user is not logged in, it also proceeds to create a new user.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-user-002 title: user login module: user source pages: 1 preconditions: user is registered. test data: - email: user@example.com - password: password123 test steps: 1. open the application. 2. click on the login button. 3. enter the provided email and password. 4. submit the form. expected result: the user should be logged in successfully and redirected to the home page.

item_text: the main flow starts with the app being launched and checks if the user is logged in.

item_text: if the user is logged in, it proceeds to check if the user is viewing a city's details.
best_testcase_text: test case id: tc-city-001 title: search for a city module: city source pages: 3, 4 preconditions: user is logged in. test data: - city name: rome test steps: 1. navigate to the city search screen. 2. enter the provided city name. 3. submit the search query. expected result: the city view screen for rome should be displayed.

item_text: if the user is viewing a city's details, it allows the user to scroll down and add an event.

item_text: the user then fills out the form and validates the fields before creating the event.
